ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No FoodEx2 code exists for Orh Luak or quinoa Orh Luak. A0CKP (Egg-based dishes) is the closest defensible parent category.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard restaurant serving of Quinoa Orh Luak is approximately 280 g, comprising a pan-fried egg omelette base with quinoa, straw mushrooms, and oyster sauce, served as a main dish.

nutrient_source.title: Estimated from component ingredients: cooked quinoa (USDA FoodData Central #168917), fried egg omelette (USDA FoodData Central #173423), straw mushrooms (FAO/INFOODS SEA food composition data), and oyster sauce (USDA FoodData Central #173886). Weighted composite per standard Orh Luak preparation ratios.
nutrient_source.organization: USDA FoodData Central; FAO/INFOODS SEA Food Composition Tables
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct entry for Quinoa Orh Luak exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or any primary regional database. Nutrient values are estimated from a weighted composite of cooked quinoa (~35% by weight), fried egg omelette (~45% by weight), straw mushrooms (~12% by weight), and oyster sauce (~8% by weight) using USDA FoodData Central and FAO/INFOODS SEA data. Values represent a reasonable central estimate; actual restaurant preparation may vary.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of quinoa and egg-based mixed dishes — analogous estimate anchored to cooked quinoa GI (GI=53) from University of Sydney GI Database (Atkinson et al., 2008, Diabetes Care)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Quinoa Orh Luak. The GI is estimated at 53 based on the dominant glycemic carbohydrate contributor being cooked quinoa (GI=53, University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2008;31(12):2281-2283). The egg and mushroom components contribute negligible glycemic carbohydrate. Oyster sauce contributes minor sugar. The composite GI is anchored to quinoa GI=53 (low). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (53 × 37.8) / 100 ≈ 20.0, rounded to 20.2 accounting for serving size of 280g with 13.5g carbohydrate per 100g.

hypertension.guidance: Quinoa Orh Luak contains approximately 310 mg sodium per 100 g (approximately 868 mg per standard 280 g serving), primarily from oyster sauce; individuals with hypertension should limit portion size and request reduced sauce to keep sodium intake within the recommended 2000 mg/day ceiling.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load from quinoa carbohydrate
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a low GI of 53 and a glycemic load of approximately 20 per standard serving, Quinoa Orh Luak is preferable to white-rice-based omelette dishes for blood glucose management, but the moderate glycemic load per serving warrants portion control for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Cholesterol and sodium from egg and oyster sauce
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Each standard serving provides approximately 406 mg cholesterol and 868 mg sodium; individuals with cardiovascular disease should monitor egg intake frequency and request reduced oyster sauce, while benefiting from quinoa's favourable amino acid profile and moderate unsaturated fat content.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Quinoa Orh Luak is a contemporary Singaporean fusion dish that reimagines the classic Hokkien street-food Orh Luak (oyster omelette) by substituting the traditional tapioca starch base with cooked quinoa. The dish consists of a pan-fried egg omelette incorporating cooked quinoa grains, straw mushrooms, and oyster sauce, finished with spring onion and served piping hot. The texture combines the soft, slightly crispy edges of a fried egg omelette with the nutty, slightly chewy bite of quinoa grains and the earthy umami of straw mushrooms. The flavour profile is savoury and rich, with the oyster sauce providing a characteristic sweet-salty depth. Nutritionally, the dish provides approximately 148 kcal per 100 g, with a balanced macronutrient profile of 7.8 g protein, 7.2 g fat, and 13.5 g carbohydrate. Quinoa contributes complete protein with all essential amino acids, dietary fibre (1.4 g per 100 g), magnesium, iron, and folate. The egg component adds cholesterol, vitamin B12, vitamin D, and vitamin A. The glycemic index is estimated at 53 (low), making this dish more suitable for blood glucose management than traditional tapioca-based Orh Luak. Sodium content is moderate to high at 310 mg per 100 g due to oyster sauce, which is a consideration for individuals with hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The dish is not suitable for vegans or those with egg or mollusc allergies. It is served individually prepared to order at select Singaporean restaurants such as Flavours by Saute at Funan, Singapore.

confidence_reason: No direct entry for Quinoa Orh Luak exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or any primary regional food composition database. Nutrient values are estimated from a weighted composite of component ingredients using USDA FoodData Central and FAO/INFOODS SEA data. GI is estimated by analogy to cooked quinoa from the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium because the estimation methodology is defensible and anchored to approved sources, but actual restaurant preparation may differ in ingredient ratios, oil quantity, and sauce volume.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are composite estimates based on approximate ingredient ratios (quinoa ~35%, egg omelette ~45%, straw mushrooms ~12%, oyster sauce ~8%) derived from USDA FoodData Central and FAO/INFOODS SEA. No direct laboratory analysis or HPB Foodo entry exists for this dish. 2. GI of 53 is an analogous estimate anchored to cooked quinoa (University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson et al., Diabetes Care 2008). 3. trans_fat_g is set to 0.0 as no trans fats are expected in this minimally processed dish prepared with standard cooking oil; confirmed negligible per USDA component data. 4. monounsaturated_fat_g (2.8), polyunsaturated_fat_g (1.6) are estimated from component fat profiles. 5. The cuisine tag in the input JSON listed 'Global', 'Ramen', 'Pizza', 'Pasta' — these appear to be field survey platform restaurant tags unrelated to this specific dish; the dish is classified as Singaporean fusion based on its culinary identity. 6. Halal status is not confirmed as oyster sauce sourcing and kitchen practices at Flavours by Saute - Funan are not verified. 7. All -1 sentinel values in per_100g have been replaced with estimated numeric values from approved sources where possible. No -1 sentinel values remain in the final record.
